q: Two trains 110 km/h and 150 km/h are running towards each other in opposite directions. The length of one train is 500 m. To completely pass each other it takes 10 seconds. Find the length of the second train.
An article is sold at a loss of 10% for Rs. 2,700. Find its cost price.
If 17, 9, 26 and x are added to give the resulting numbers in proportion, then x : y :: (4x-2) and y > 0, find the value of x.

The price of an AC machine and a TV is the same. If the price of the AC machine is increased by 50% and the price of the TV is decreased by 28%, then the price of the AC machine becomes how much more than the price of the TV?
Simplify (3x-2)^2 - (x+4)^2.
If 3/7 of a certain number is 60% of 615, then find that number.
A shopkeeper marks the price of an article at Rs. 1,980 above its cost price. If after giving a 12% discount he still gains 32%, then find the cost price of the article.
The sides of a triangle are 68 cm, 31 cm, and 87 cm. What is the length of its altitude corresponding to the side with a length of 31 cm?
A train 1386 m long is running at a speed of 18 km/h. How long will it take to completely cross a platform 564 m long?
If 5/13 of a certain number is added to 7/39 of the same number, the result is 132. Find the number.
A shopkeeper sold a pen for Rs. 150 and earned a profit of Rs. 30. Find the cost price of the pen.
A man and his son have ages such that 6 years ago the man's age was 8 times his son's age, and now the man's age is 30 years more than his son's. What is their present total age (in years)?

Pipes M and N can fill a tank in 36 hours and 45 hours respectively. A pipe P can empty the filled tank in 30 hours. First M and N are opened together and after 5 hours both are closed, then P is opened. After how many hours will the tank become empty?
Today the price of 1 kg of mango is Rs. 40. If in the next two days its price increases by 5% and 3% respectively, then what will be its price after two days?
An article is sold at 4% below its marked price, giving a profit of 20%. By what percentage above the cost price is the marked price of the article marked?

A, B and C respectively deposit money at 3.5% and 7.5% annual rates of interest. To pay back Rs. 2,00,000 in 2 years, money is deposited in two different banks. Find the total simple interest (in Rs.) given to both banks.
The data 30, 33, 49, 51, x, x+4, 73, 79, 85, 96 is arranged in ascending order and the median of the data is 64. Find the value of x.
Find the simplified value of: (1 - secx) / (cscx - cotx)
The ratio of two numbers is 2 : 5, and their highest common factor (HCF) is 29. Then their lowest common multiple (LCM) is:
A and B together can complete a piece of work in 2 days. If B alone can complete the same work in 10 days, then in how many days will A alone complete the same work (in days)?
If Rs. 8,604 is divided between two persons in the ratio 7 : 15, then find the share (in Rs.) of one of them.
The average age of 37 students in a class is 42 years. If the teacher's age is also included, the average age of the whole group increases by 1 year. Find the teacher's age.
If the radius of a right circular cylinder is decreased by 12% and its height is increased by 169%, then what is the percentage increase (closest integer) in its volume?
If 'A' means '+', 'B' means '-', 'C' means 'x' and 'D' means '÷', then what will come in place of the following: 16 B 3 D 36 A 18 C 18 = ?

In the following number, figures are arranged in such a way that each pair gives the same answer. How many such pairs are there: 0 9 % 6 1 * E 5 # 2 8 5 7 & 6 3 4 (in pairs)? (each pair in which the first number comes immediately before the second number and the number comes immediately after)

On the basis of the dictionary's word order, which of the following four words will come in third place? (Note: assemble-meaningless words and the number of vowels-consonants are not based on their position)

Considering the context of the following number sequence, answer the question given below (all numbers are single-digit). Which figure will come immediately after the figure that comes immediately before the figure that gives an odd answer: 8 7 7 4 2 5 8 4 8 5 8 9 7 3 5 4 (in odd)?
In a certain code language, 'mall tree army' is written as 'vg ur ua' and 'plant army cup' is written as 'ti ud vg', then how is 'army' written in that code language?

Considering the given statements to be true (even if they seem to differ from commonly known facts), decide which of the conclusions given below logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: I) All trees are mountains. II) All mountains are rivers. III) All rivers are dams. Conclusions: (i) All trees are dams. (ii) Some dams are mountains.
In the following, which number-letter group, considering the logic, will complete the series to make it appropriate: ANF 17, DQI 21, GTL 25, JWO 29, ?

On the basis of the dictionary's word order, which of the following is related in the same way: GENU is related to KZRP, similarly OUVK is related to SPZF. In the same manner, following the same logic, WKDA is related to which of the given options?

Considering the context of the following number sequence and figures, answer the question given below (all numbers are single-digit). $ # & E 9 % E 7 * & E 1 3 4 5 6 4 9 E 2 5 & (in DAY). Which figure comes immediately before the figure that comes immediately after a figure?

On the basis of the dictionary's word order, which of the following four words will come in third place? (Note: assemble-meaningless words and the number of vowels-consonants are not based on their position)
What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following series: 319 320 323 328 335 ?

Considering the context of the following number sequence and answering the question given below (all numbers are single-digit). 4 1 1 7 2 4 1 2 7 4 4 9 6 3 9 1 6 1 9 2 2 8 6 (in DAY). Which figure that comes immediately before the figure that comes immediately after a figure is odd?
If 'A' means '+', 'B' means '-', 'C' means 'x' and 'D' means '÷', then what will come in place of the following: 44 A 11 B 25 D 28 C 1 = ?
DR 12 is related to FK -6 in a certain way. In the same way JM 18, LF 0 is related. Following the same logic, KN 3 is related to which of the given options?

In the figure, a, c, b, e, f, x and z, one above the other, are kept in a box. If b is just below a and just above e, and the box also contains c and f, and f is below e, then which box is just below c?

Out of the given options, which number pair, in a certain way, is similar to the number pairs shown below? (Note: operations should be done on the entire number, without breaking the number into its constituent digits, e.g., do not perform operations after adding 13 and 14; an operation such as adding 13 to or subtracting 13 from etc. is not allowed.) (35 - 24, 1) (35, 14, 7)
In the following, which letter group, considering the logic, will complete the series to make it appropriate: CFE10, GJI14, KNM18, ?

In a certain code language, P + Q means 'A', P - Q means 'B', P x Q means 'C' and P ÷ Q means 'D'. If P A Q B R means E, what is the relation of Q to S? If Q is P and S is M, then how are S and B related?
o: S is the mother of B
S is the father of B
S is the son of B
S is the brother of B

q: Considering the given statements to be true (even if they seem to differ from commonly known facts), decide which of the conclusions given below logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All apples are pears. All pears are guavas. Some grapes are apples. Conclusions: (I) Some guavas are apples. (II) Some grapes are guavas.
